text: The surgeon injects a solution into the fibroid capsule, utilizing aqua dissection to facilitate the release of the fibroid from the uterine cavity and bed.

The surgeon prepares to suture the large bed left by the removal of the anterior intramural fibroid, employing a multilayered suturing technique using a quill suture, which eliminates the need for tying knots.
The surgeon uses baseball sutures to take the deeper layer of the uterine tissue, aiming to obliterate the bed of the myoma and achieve hemostasis.
The surgeon demonstrates ipsilateral baseball suturing at the myoma bed, showing no visible evidence of hematoma formation in the sutured area.
The surgeon completes suturing the deeper layer of the myoma bed and begins suturing the external layer using baseball sutures, ensuring the outer layer is securely closed.
The surgeon demonstrates the overlapping technique of suturing the external surface of the myoma bed, minimizing contact between the sutures and the raw area with the peritoneum.
The surgeon applies gentle traction while pulling the suture, ensuring the tissue approximates along the suture line in the myoma bed.
The surgeon removes the suture material, ensuring the knot prevents any slippage. The surgical field reveals a bloodless bed of the myoma, indicating complete suturing.
